extension.memoEdit.title: Memo: List/Edit
extension.memoQuick.title: Memo: Today's quick Memo
extension.memoGrep.title: Memo: Grep
extension.memoServe.title: Memo: Serve
extension.memoConfig.title: Memo: Config
extension.memoReDate.title: Memo: Re:Date
extension.memoTodo.title: Memo: Todo
extension.memoOpenFolder.title: Memo: Open Memo Folder in New Instance
extension.memoOpenChrome.title: Memo: Open Chrome with <html contenteditable>
extension.memoOpenTypora.title: Memo: Open Typora
extension.memoAdmin.title: Memo: Admin
memoPath.desc: Absolute path to the memo command
titlePrefix.desc: When executing `Memo: Today's Quick Memo`, it automatically inserts the specified prefix string of the title
dateFormat.desc: Follow format of date-fns. See: https://date-fns.org/v2.16.1/docs/format
insertISOWeek.desc: When executing `Memo: Today's Quick Memo`, it automatically inserts ISO Week number in the title
insertEmoji.desc: When executing `Memo: Today's Quick Memo`, it automatically inserts random-emoji in the title
gutterIconPath.desc: When executing `Memo: Grep`, the PATH to the icon displayed in the gutter
gutterIconSize.desc: When executing `Memo: Grep`, the size of icon displayed in gutter (default: 100%)
displayFileBirthTime.desc: Display birthtime of file in `Memo: Edit` additionally
withRespectMode.desc: Change appearance of search results. © 2011 Microsoft Corporation All Rights Reserved.」
grepLineBackgroundColor.desc: Background color of search result keywords
grepKeywordBackgroundColor.desc: Background color of line containing search result keywords
listMarkdownPreview.desc: Markdown Preview is displayed at the same time when selecting a file. However, `Markdown Preview Enhanced`(https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=shd101wyy.markdown-preview-enhanced) Extension must be installed.
openMarkdownPreview.desc: Open built-in Markdown Preview at the same time as opening the file in the editor. If `Markdown Preview Enhanced` Extension (https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=shd101wyy.markdown-preview-enhanced) is installed and `openMarkdownPreviewUseMPE` is set to `true`, use it.
listSortOrder.desc: Controls the order of Memo: List display. Selectable from `filename` or `birthtime` or `mtime`
memoGrepUseRipGrepConfigFile.desc: Do you want ripgrep to work with the configuration file (.ripgreprc)
memoGrepViewMode.desc: Choose how `Memo: Grep` displays results. `quickPick` shows a dropdown list, `outputChannel` shows results in a text-style output pane, `readOnlyDocument` opens the results in a temporary read-only document, `editableDocument` opens an editable unsaved document.
memoGrepUseRipGrepConfigFilePath.desc: If you set `memoGrepUseRipGrepConfigFile` to `ture` and want to use ripgrep config file located further in a specific place, set the path of config file
memoTodoUserePattern.desc: Define a pattern to recognize as todo (default: ^.*@todo.*?:)
memoNewFilenameFromClipboard.desc: Use a copy of the OS clipboard as the name of the newly created file.
memoNewFilenameFromSelection.desc: Use the selected string in the active editor as the name of the newly created file
memoNewFilNameDateSuffix.desc: Add a date-related suffix after the file name (yyyy-MM-dd). The added string is passed to datefns.format (). See https://date-fns.org/v2.16.1/docs/format for more information.
memoTemplatesDir.desc: Directory path for memo templates. Defaults to the `.templates` folder inside the memo config directory. Override per-workspace to use project-local templates.
memoSnippetsDir.desc: Directory path for memo snippets (VS Code snippet JSON format). Defaults to the `.snippets` folder inside the memo directory. Override per-workspace to use project-local snippets.
memoDatePathFormat.desc: Create date-based subfolders under memodir when saving memo files. Example: `yyyy/MM` or `yyyy/MM/dd`
memoAdminAppearance.desc: Set the appearance mode used by the Memo: Admin screen. Choose from `system`, `light`, or `dark`.
memoAdminColorTheme.desc: Set the accent color theme used by the Memo: Admin screen. Choose from `blue`, `teal`, `amber`, `rose`, `mono`, or `forest`.
memoDisplayLanguage.desc: Choose the UI language used by Memo screens. `auto` follows the VS Code display language.
memoAdminUseGradient.desc: Enable the accent gradient background on the Memo: Admin screen.
memoAdminOpenMode.desc:
Choose how `Memo: Admin` opens. `currentWindow` uses the current VS Code window, `newWindow` opens the memo root as the top-level folder in a dedicated window.

Keyboard shortcuts are edited from the Keyboard Shortcuts editor or `keybindings.json`.
memoAdminOpenOnStartup.desc:
Open `Memo: Admin` automatically when VS Code starts for this window or workspace.

Keyboard shortcuts are edited from the Keyboard Shortcuts editor or `keybindings.json`.
memoPinnedFiles.desc: Pinned memo file paths shown in the Memo: Admin screen.
memoConfDir.desc: Directory path used for memo configuration files.
openMarkdownPreviewUseMPE.desc: If `memo-life-for-you.openMarkdownPreview` is set to `true`, use `Markdown Preview Enhanced` (https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=shd101wyy.markdown-preview-enhanced) to open preview (default: false)
openChromeCustomizeURL.desc: Customize Chrome's URL to be launched with Memo: Open Chrome with <html contenteditable> command.
openTyporaExecPath.desc: The extension of the file to be displayed in the Memo:List/Edit.
memolistDisplayExtname.desc: The extension of the file to be displayed in the Memo:List/Edit.
extension.memoAutoTag.title: Memo: AI - Auto Tag
extension.memoSummarize.title: Memo: AI - Summarize
extension.memoRelated.title: Memo: AI - Related Memos
extension.memoExtractTodos.title: Memo: AI - Extract Todos
extension.memoReport.title: Memo: AI - Generate Report
extension.memoProofread.title: Memo: AI - Proofread
extension.memoQA.title: Memo: AI - Q&A
extension.memoSuggestTemplate.title: Memo: AI - Suggest Template
extension.memoGenerateTitle.title: Memo: AI - Generate Title
extension.memoTranslate.title: Memo: AI - Translate
extension.memoLinkSuggest.title: Memo: AI - Link Suggest
aiEnabled.desc: Enable AI features (auto-tag, summarize, translate, etc.). When disabled, all AI commands are hidden from the command palette and context menu. No external API calls are made.
aiProvider.desc: LLM provider for AI features. `ollama` for local Ollama, `openai` for any OpenAI-compatible API (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Groq, Together AI, Mistral, DeepSeek, LiteLLM, vLLM, LocalAI, etc.). Note: Anthropic Claude and Google Gemini can also be used via LiteLLM with the `openai` provider.
aiEndpoint.desc: API endpoint URL. Ollama default: `http://localhost:11434`. For OpenAI: `https://api.openai.com/v1`. For Claude: `https://api.anthropic.com`.
aiModel.desc: Model name to use for tag generation. Ollama example: `gemma3:4b`. OpenAI example: `gpt-4o-mini`.
aiApiKey.desc: API key for cloud providers (not needed for Ollama).
aiTagLanguage.desc: Language for AI responses and generated tags. `auto` follows VS Code display language, `ja` for Japanese, `en` for English.
aiProxy.desc: HTTP proxy URL for AI API requests (e.g. `http://proxy.example.com:8080`). Leave empty to connect directly.
aiProxyBypass.desc: Equivalent to the `NO_PROXY` environment variable. Comma-separated list of hostnames that should bypass the proxy (e.g. `localhost,127.0.0.1`). Supports domain suffixes (e.g. `.local`) and `*` for all hosts.
aiTlsRejectUnauthorized.desc: Reject unauthorized TLS certificates. Set to `false` to allow self-signed certificates (e.g. corporate proxy with MITM inspection).
aiTlsCaCert.desc: Path to a custom CA certificate file (PEM format) for TLS verification. Used when connecting through a corporate proxy with a custom CA.
